message:
feat(3d): add Web Worker for structure parsing and enhance rendering

Introduces a dedicated Web Worker for offloading heavy PDB/mmCIF parsing
and significantly improves the 3D protein structure viewer with enhanced
lighting, auto-quality adaptation, and modern rendering techniques.

Web Worker Integration (structure.worker.ts):
- Offload expensive parsing operations (PDB/mmCIF format detection and
  atom extraction) to a background thread to prevent UI blocking
- Move bond detection algorithm (O(N²) neighbor search) off main thread
- Compute backbone traces for ribbon visualization in worker
- Detect functional groups (aromatic rings, disulfide bonds, phosphate
  groups) without blocking renders
- Calculate bounding sphere (center + radius) for camera framing
- Transfer serialized data using structured clone for performance

Structure Loader Refactor (structure-loader.ts):
- Replace inline parsePDB/parseMMCIF with worker-based loading
- Import worker using Vite's `?worker` syntax for proper bundling
- Maintain same public API (loadStructure, buildBallAndStick, etc.)
- Update ELEMENT_COLORS with slightly brighter carbon (#94a3b8) for
  better visibility on dark backgrounds

Model3DView Rendering Enhancements:
- Enable ACES Filmic tone mapping with 1.12 exposure for cinematic look
- Add hemisphere light (sky: #c3ddff, ground: #0d1326) for natural
  ambient illumination
- Reposition key/fill/rim lights with increased intensities for better
  depth perception
- Add "headlamp" light attached to camera position for visibility from
  all viewing angles
- Introduce fog (color: #0f1b33, near: 120, far: 2400) for atmospheric
  depth
- Update scene background from #0b1021 to #0f1b33 to match fog

Auto-Quality Adaptation:
- Start at 'high' quality preset and automatically adapt based on FPS
- Downgrade quality if FPS drops below 20 (ultra→high→medium→low)
- Upgrade quality if FPS exceeds 55 for smooth performance headroom
- Sample FPS every 1 second (increased from 500ms) for stability
- Remove manual quality cycling in favor of automatic adaptation

Performance Impact:
- Heavy parsing no longer blocks UI interactions or causes jank
- Smooth 60fps maintained during structure loading on capable devices
- Graceful degradation on lower-end hardware through auto-quality

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
